envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Tool Map intake feeds connector readiness + activation gating. Entitlement makes a tool available; admin approval + per-region Map approval + per-connector RULE 21 research arc authorize activation per ADR-0084.

items.name: Tool Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Tool Map. Maps which tools the company / department / user actually uses, which contain sensitive data, which surfaces are read-first-safe, and which connector activations the admin approves. Composes against Wave 6 connector-priority matrix.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks what tools you use, who admins them, what they contain. Outputs Tool Map candidates pending admin approval. Entitlement makes a connector available; governance authorizes activation.
items.model_usage_notes: Use connector-priority matrix (docs/ootb-catalog/connector-priority-matrix.json) as a starter prior. Always read-first by default. Write actions disabled until explicitly delegated. Risky writes require dual-control per ADR-0026.
required_questions: What tools does the company use (company-wide stack)?
What tools does the department use (department-specific)?
What tools do you (the user) use personally for work?
Who admins each tool (admin / billing owner / IT)?
What data does each tool contain (operational / customer-PII / financial / legal / health / etc.)?
optional_questions: What are the read surfaces (search / list / view)?
What are the write surfaces (create / update / delete / send)?
What are the risky actions (legal-effect / money-moving / external-facing)?
Which tools have OAuth / enterprise-admin / SCIM support?
Who owns OAuth / security for each tool?
Which tools are forbidden for initial rollout (legal / sensitive / regulated)?
admin_questions: Which connector packs should the Map propose at initial activation?
Which tools must NOT be in any connector pack proposal (sensitive / regulated / pre-approval-only)?
Which tools require dual-control writes by policy?
Which tools have an existing data-handling policy that connector activation must respect?
user_questions: Does the Map represent your tool usage accurately?
Are there tools you use that should NOT be in scope?
evidence_sources: Wave 2.1 role-depth files (per-role tool usage) at docs/ootb-catalog/role-depth/
output_candidates: Tool Map proposal (company / department / user-tool inventory)
governance_review_points: Admin must approve every connector activation per ADR-0084
Per-region Map approval per ADR-0082 Amendment 1 remains stronger than entitlement
Per-connector RULE 21 research arc required before real adapter implementation
Risky writes mandatory dual-control per ADR-0026
safe_defaults: OOTB connector presets as starter priors
forbidden_defaults: Connector activation from billing entitlement alone
DMW_scope_implications: Tool Map bounds which connector data can flow into DMW memory
Forbidden tools cannot scope any DMW without explicit later admin authorization
Risky-write tools require Authority Map approval before any write-side DMW provenance
Per-role tool-usage from Wave 2.1 §24 constrains per-role DMW scope
audit_expectations: future INVOKE_CONNECTOR + ACTION_* at runtime per ADR-0084
